api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.title: Class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions | Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Documentation
api/Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.DbC.summary: Class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Namespace Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data Assembly Wolfgang.Extensions.Logging.Data.dll Extension methods on ILogger for logging DbConnection instances. public static class DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Inheritance object DbConnectionLoggerExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The ConnectionString is parsed via DbConnectionStringBuilder and the Password and Pwd keys are removed before logging so credentials are never written to the log. Key matching is case-insensitive per the DbConnectionStringBuilder contract, so password, PASSWORD, etc. are all redacted. All other keys, including the user name (User ID, UID, etc.), are preserved. Methods LogDbConnection(ILogger, DbConnection) Logs a single structured entry describing the connection at LogLevel.Information. public static void LogDbConnection(this ILogger logger, DbConnection connection) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. connection DbConnection The connection to log. Examples using var connection = new SqlConnection(connectionString); await connection.OpenAsync(); logger.LogDbConnection(connection); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or connection is null. LogDbConnection(ILogger, DbConnection, LogLevel) Logs a single structured entry describing the connection at the specified level. public static void LogDbConnection(this ILogger logger, DbConnection connection, LogLevel level) Parameters logger ILogger The logger to write to. connection DbConnection The connection to log. level LogLevel The log level to write the entry at. Examples using var connection = new SqlConnection(connectionString); await connection.OpenAsync(); logger.LogDbConnection(connection, LogLevel.Debug); Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown when logger or connection is null.
